name: ETH Drive Continuation v1
thesis: ETH 4h is the best-fit primary lane for a broadened drive-continuation thesis under the current chop-leaning regime. The edge should come from accepted directional bursts that hold structure for a few bars, not from exact breakout-candle timing.
invalidation_condition: Abort if drive-and-hold setups remain too sparse, if persistence and hold filters conflict, or if continuation fails to survive costs in blind windows.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional burst establishes local dominance and the next bar or two hold enough structure for continuation instead of immediate mean reversion.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in a mostly choppy 4h regime, accepted directional bursts can persist briefly when the first retrace fails to reclaim prior balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the cleanest lane for this thesis because the live program now centers on ETH 4h and the mechanism needs enough structure to distinguish a real hold from intrabar noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If ETH remains purely rotational without brief persistence bursts, hold entries will degrade into chop losses.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries occur but persistence is too short, shorten the holding horizon and de-risk faster before tightening the trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; momentum improvement and structural hold are aligned
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad drive-hold definitions instead of exact opening-range rules

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14
